join_order.notes: sub_fk is dense uint32_t index into sub table; sub_to_group[sub_fk] returns group_id or -1 for non-qualifying rows

scan.notes: Only scan [usdStart, usdEnd) range via mmap + madvise MADV_SEQUENTIAL on the needed range

data_structures.sub_to_group: int32_t[86135] — sub_row → group_id or -1 if fy!=2022; 344KB fits L2
data_structures.fy2022_bitmap: uint8_t[86135] — 1 byte per sub row for branch-free check; 86KB fits L2. Set to 1 if fy==2022, 0 otherwise. Use as fast pre-filter before group_id lookup.
data_structures.cik_to_name: flat array mapping: groups vector indexed by group_id, built during dim_filter
data_structures.aggregation: double[num_groups] per thread — thread-local dense arrays. ~10K groups × 8 bytes = 80KB per thread. 64 threads × 80KB = 5MB total, fits LLC. CRITICAL: use double, NOT long double. long double uses x87 FPU and prevents vectorization.

parallelism.notes: 33M rows across 64 threads. Thread-local double arrays for aggregation. Merge phase: parallelize with each thread reducing a slice of group_ids across all thread-local arrays.

aggregation.key: int32_t group_id (dense, maps to unique (name, cik) pair)
aggregation.notes: Group by (name, cik) using dense group_id assigned during dim_filter. Same CIK can have different names. HAVING subquery re-aggregates by cik after main aggregation. Use double precision throughout — do NOT use long double.

optimization_notes: Category B (physical implementation mismatch): The iter_0 inner loop used long double (80-bit x87 FPU) for accumulation, which prevents SSE/AVX vectorization and is 3-5x slower per operation than double. With ~6.5M qualifying rows in the hot loop, this is the dominant cost. Fix: (1) Replace all long double with double throughout — aggregation arrays, merge, threshold computation. This enables auto-vectorization of the inner accumulation loop. (2) Use madvise(MADV_SEQUENTIAL) on the scanned range of sub_fk.bin and value.bin instead of MAP_POPULATE on entire files — reduces page fault overhead. (3) Parallelize the merge phase: instead of single-threaded iteration over 64×10K entries, each thread reduces a slice of group_ids. (4) Increase morsel size to 128K for better instruction cache utilization and reduced scheduling overhead. (5) Use a separate uint8_t bitmap for fy2022 qualification check (86KB, fits L2) for branch-free pre-filtering before group_id lookup. Format output with %.2f for double.
